title: 6 Settlement of a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
body_text:
6	Settlement of a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account
6.1	Summary of Provincial Concentrator Holding Account Settlement
Settlement of Cheques Processed in Branch Capture
Central 1 credits your financial institutionâs Central 1 account for the value of cheques processed in Branch Capture. Branch Capture deposits appear on your financial institutionâs statement (AIBC/AIBU for BC, AIOC/AIOU for Ontario) as code âBCâ.
Central 1 may issue a deposit adjustment for cheques processed via Branch Capture if
an item was deposited that canât be processed (e.g., image quality problems, piggybacks);
an item amount was keyed incorrectly, and a deposit was force balanced; or
an item was received that shouldnât have been included in the deposit (e.g., the currency of the item doesnât match the currency of the deposit type, the item isnât eligible for clearing).
See the Deposit Processing Part for more information on deposit adjustments.
Settlement for Provincial Concentrator Holding Accounts
When Central 1 initiates the sweep, a Provincial Concentrator holding account is debited for the deposit total(s) that you report on the Province of BC Deposit Slip in ServiceNow by pre-authorized payment (PAD). These transactions appear on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report. The account balance should zero at the end of each processing cycle. Central 1 assesses the total value to apply to the Province of BCâs account held at Central 1.
You must monitor and reconcile a Provincial Concentrator holding account daily. If thereâs a problem with a pre-authorized debit (PAD), donât return the PAD through the clearing. If an error is identified, submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request form in ServiceNow. See Section 5.2, Managing Deposit Errors for a Provincial Concentrator Holding Account, for procedures.
END
